market_summary: U.S. markets are mixed Tuesday with the S&P 500 near flat (+0.11%) and the Nasdaq modestly positive (+0.33%), while large-cap AI/tech names face sector-wide pressure from regulatory headwinds and profit-taking; NVIDIA's Computex/GTC Taipei announcements are the dominant AI sector catalyst of the day.

MSFT.headline: FTC has broadened its investigation into Microsoft's cloud, AI, and software bundling practices, weighing on the stock alongside elevated AI capex concerns at Build 2026.
MSFT.reasoning: The FTC investigation is a genuine watch item, but regulatory probes of this nature typically take years to resolve and rarely result in breakup or material revenue impairment for diversified cloud leaders. On fundamentals, Q3 FY2026 delivered $61.9B revenue (+13% YoY), Azure +31%, and 80%+ Fortune 500 penetration of Azure AI — the core thesis remains firmly intact. Build 2026 announcements today on next-gen Copilot agents represent continued monetization expansion; long-term holders should sit tight and monitor FTC developments without alarm.

AMZN.headline: Bank of America initiates with a Buy rating, calling Amazon's LEO satellite 'Project Kuiper' on the cusp of generating revenues in Q3 2026, adding a new growth catalyst to AWS.
AMZN.reasoning: Amazon is up 19% YTD, outpacing the S&P 500's 10% gain, with Q1 results showing accelerating cloud demand and a fast-growing advertising segment. AWS remains the dominant profit engine and the agentic AI shift is a structural tailwind. The BofA Buy initiation on Kuiper represents incremental upside for a long-term holder; no action needed.

NVDA.headline: Jensen Huang's GTC Taipei keynote unveiled the Vera Rubin AI computing platform, the standalone 'Vera' data center CPU, and the RTX Spark AI PC chip, cementing Nvidia's expansion across every layer of the AI stack.
NVDA.reasoning: Nvidia is the clearest infrastructure beneficiary of the AI buildout cycle — its CEO's Computex keynote showcased Vera Rubin and RTX Spark, broadening its addressable market from data centers into AI PCs while Cathie Wood purchased 300K shares today. With a $5.48T market cap, a P/E of 34.6x, and a quarterly dividend ex-date on June 4, the stock remains a core AI holding; today's +2.34% move is a healthy reaction to product catalysts. Hold confidently.

GOOGL.headline: Alphabet filed to raise up to $80 billion in a common stock offering to fund AI infrastructure expansion, triggering modest dilution concern and intraday volatility.
GOOGL.reasoning: A planned $80B equity raise is material and warrants monitoring — it signals aggressive AI capex commitment and will cause near-term dilution. However, Alphabet began 2026 with $110B in Q1 revenue (+22% YoY) and Google Cloud up 63% to $20B, giving it the fundamental firepower to absorb the dilution. With 56 analysts rating it Strong Buy and a 12-month consensus target of ~$430 vs. today's ~$374, long-term holders should hold and watch how the capital raise is received over coming weeks.

alerts.message: FTC has broadened its investigation into Microsoft's cloud, AI, and software bundling practices — monitor for escalation, but no action warranted at this stage.
Alphabet filed to raise up to $80B in common stock — watch for pricing terms and market reception over the next 1-2 weeks to assess dilution impact.

long_term_view: All four holdings remain at the epicenter of the secular AI infrastructure and cloud computing buildout, with no fundamental thesis breaks today — the FTC probe on MSFT and GOOGL's equity raise are the only items requiring ongoing monitoring. Sector-wide capex commitments ($190B from MSFT, $200B from AMZN) and NVDA's relentless product cadence reinforce the multi-year earnings growth runway for patient long-term holders.
